name: Valdai Discussion Club
why: Elite Russian strategic think-tank. Editorial positions preview Russian foreign policy framing before it appears in state media. Divergence from Western framing on same events IS the intelligence signal.

name: Observer Research Foundation (ORF India)
why: India's leading strategic think-tank. Non-Western, non-Chinese perspective — third pole analytical tradition. Indo-Pacific, multipolarity, South-South framing of same events as Western sources. India's strategic autonomy doctrine produces distinctly different geopolitical readings.

name: Institute for Security Studies (ISS Africa)
why: Premier African security think-tank. African analytical tradition on governance, conflict, and state fragility differs substantially from Western framing. Reads the same African and global events through the lens of colonialism's structural legacy — which Western analysis systematically misses.

name: ISEAS Yusof Ishak Institute (ASEAN Studies)
why: Singapore-based. Southeast Asian strategic perspective on US-China competition, ASEAN centrality, and regional order. ASEAN framing explicitly avoids forced binary alignment — the most important analytical tradition for understanding fence-sitter geopolitics that both US and Chinese narratives systematically distort.

name: Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
why: Has offices in Moscow, Beijing, Beirut, Brussels, New Delhi, Berlin. The non-Washington Carnegie offices produce analysis from genuinely local perspectives — different from Western MSM. Carnegie Moscow and Carnegie China are especially valuable for non-adversarial expert framing of Russian and Chinese strategic thinking.
